summary: Operate as August's main OpenClaw agent.
decision_mode: Makes bounded operational decisions, but asks or refuses for external/public/destructive actions.
needs_august_review: Yes for external sends, security exposure, token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAME rotation, destructive changes, or public bridge/MCP approval.
capabilities: Operate as August's main OpenClaw agent.
Use the OpenAI Codex runtime.
Read and modify local workspace files when requested.
Run local commands and use OpenClaw messaging/tools.
Coordinate project work, reporting, and recovery workflows.

summary: Intended target for governed memory review and Codex-memory tasks.
decision_mode: Candidate decision-maker for memory operations; destructive or mutating memory work requires review/approval.
needs_august_review: Yes for memory mutation, restore, prune, delete, promotion, or cloud recall.
capabilities: Intended target for governed memory review and Codex-memory tasks.
Has an OpenClaw agent workspace and many historical session artifacts.
Allowed as an OpenClaw hook target and bridge target.
triggers: ChatGPT OpenClaw Bridge governed tool calls.
Future MCP connector calls after approval.

summary: Named as a future or alternate governed Codex target for home Windows work.
decision_mode: Unknown; currently a configured target more than an observed active agent.
needs_august_review: Yes before treating it as production-capable.
capabilities: Named as a future or alternate governed Codex target for home Windows work.
Allowed by OpenClaw hooks and the bridge config.
No matching workspace was found in the inspected OpenClaw agent directories.

summary: Run Codex non-interactively from Windows/WSL workflows.
decision_mode: Follows prompts but can make implementation decisions inside the requested task.
capabilities: Run Codex non-interactively from Windows/WSL workflows.
Resume the latest CLI session or start a new run depending on dispatch rules.
Produce prompt, stdout, stderr, reply, and result artifacts.
triggers: Future OpenClaw hook or bridge workflows.

decision_mode: Rule-based routing; does not independently decide broader task scope.
needs_august_review: Yes before changing dispatch policy or making it send external replies.
capabilities: Poll OpenClaw webchat transcripts.
Dispatch future iPhone/WebChat messages into Codex CLI runs.
Route normal messages to resume latest Codex session.
Route planning briefs or new project messages to a new headless Codex run.
triggers: Windows logon task named OpenClaw Codex Bridge.

summary: Expose a narrow local HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:8787.
decision_mode: Policy gatekeeper; validates and rejects, but does not autonomously plan.
needs_august_review: Yes before public exposure or production MCP approval.
capabilities: Expose a narrow local HTTP API on 127.0.0.1:8787.
Authenticate tool calls with bridge token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAME headers.
Require provenance, replay timestamp, and nonce.
Forward governed tasks to OpenClaw /hooks/agent.
Reject arbitrary shell, direct memory mutation, destructive requests, vector memory, and cloud active recall.
triggers: Bridge-facing test Cloudflare tunnel during Phase 1.9.

needs_august_review: Yes before public exposure, token_REDACTED_FIELD_NAME rotation, or allowedOrigins changes.
capabilities: Serve OpenClaw Control/WebChat locally.
Host /hooks/agent for governed agent dispatch.
Manage sessions and runtime context.

summary: Provide HTTPS access from phone/WebChat to OpenClaw gateway.
needs_august_review: Yes before stopping, replacing, or treating as production.
capabilities: Provide HTTPS access from phone/WebChat to OpenClaw gateway.
Target 127.0.0.1:18789 directly.
triggers: Phone/OpenClaw dashboard access through redacted trycloudflare origin.

summary: Expose the local bridge through a temporary public-style HTTPS route.
needs_august_review: Yes before production use; current route is test-only.
capabilities: Expose the local bridge through a temporary public-style HTTPS route.
Target 127.0.0.1:8787 for Phase 1.9 testing.
triggers: Manual cloudflared quick tunnel start for Phase 1.9.

display_name: OpenClaw Bridge Rescue UI / Operator Panel
summary: Show OpenClaw gateway, bridge, Cloudflared, tunnel, WebSocket, allowed-origin, and route-classification status.
decision_mode: Displays recommendations; does not autonomously change state.
capabilities: Show OpenClaw gateway, bridge, Cloudflared, tunnel, WebSocket, allowed-origin, and route-classification status.
Document local commands for recovery operations.
Warn when public MCP is not ready.

summary: Let August interact with OpenClaw Main.
needs_august_review: No for normal direct responses; yes for external/public messages.
capabilities: Let August interact with OpenClaw Main.
Deliver visible messages through the message tool.
Carry runtime context and session state.

summary: Access OpenClaw Control through an HTTPS browser route.
capabilities: Access OpenClaw Control through an HTTPS browser route.
Serve as a mobile operator console.
triggers: August opens the current private dashboard URL/QR.
Cloudflare or future Tailscale/private route is available.

summary: Run Windows PowerShell, Node bridge, Cloudflared, watcher, Codex CLI, and project files.
decision_mode: Host only; decisions come from user or agents.
capabilities: Run Windows PowerShell, Node bridge, Cloudflared, watcher, Codex CLI, and project files.
Host the Windows side of OpenClaw/Codex workflows.

summary: Run OpenClaw gateway and agent workspaces.
capabilities: Run OpenClaw gateway and agent workspaces.
Store OpenClaw configs, sessions, and workspace memory.
Provide Linux tooling for Codex work.
triggers: WSL filesystem access from Windows scripts.

summary: Schedule recurring or one-shot OpenClaw jobs.
decision_mode: Automation only unless a scheduled agent turn runs.
needs_august_review: Yes before creating recurring external-facing automations.
capabilities: Schedule recurring or one-shot OpenClaw jobs.
Wake agents through main, isolated, current, or session-bound targets.
Support proactive checks when configured.

summary: Pair and control Android, iOS, macOS, or browser nodes when configured.
needs_august_review: Yes for pairing and sensitive device permissions.
capabilities: Pair and control Android, iOS, macOS, or browser nodes when configured.

summary: Eventually call the bridge's narrow MCP-style tools from ChatGPT.

summary: Visually represent agents, tools, routes, devices, memory stores, blockers, and workflows.
capabilities: Visually represent agents, tools, routes, devices, memory stores, blockers, and workflows.
